Quantity.name: Quantity
Quantity.docstring:
Represents a physical quantity with magnitude, unit, and uncertainty.

Examples:
    >>> from physure import Q_
    >>> length = Q_(10, "m")
    >>> time = Q_(2, "s")
    >>> velocity = length / time
    >>> print(velocity)
    5.0 m/s
    >>> print(velocity.to("km/h"))
    18.0 km/h

properties.name: _numeric_std_dev
properties.docstring: Returns the numeric standard deviation, even if std_dev returns a model.

properties.name: _has_uncertainty
properties.docstring: Checks if uncertainty is non-zero, safely handling arrays and models.

properties.name: uncertainty_model
properties.docstring: Returns the type of uncertainty model used by the core.

properties.name: uncertainty
properties.docstring: Returns the standard deviation of the uncertainty.

properties.name: unit
properties.docstring: Retrieves the unit of the quantity as a CompoundUnit.

properties.name: uncertainty_obj
properties.docstring: Returns the rich uncertainty object (legacy support).

properties.name: vector_slice
properties.docstring: Returns the vector slice if using vectorized uncertainty.

properties.name: lineage
properties.docstring: Returns the lineage dictionary if using correlated uncertainty.

methods.name: tree_flatten
methods.docstring: Flattens the Quantity for JAX Pytree registration.

methods.name: tree_unflatten
methods.docstring: Reconstructs the Quantity from JAX flatten results.

methods.name: with_system
methods.docstring: Returns a new Quantity bound to a different unit system.

methods.name: simplify
methods.docstring: Simplifies the unit of the quantity into the system's preferred form.

methods.name: from_input
methods.signature: from_input(cls, value, unit, system, uncertainty, symbol)
methods.docstring: Creates a Quantity from raw input values.

methods.name: to_hdf5
methods.docstring: Saves the quantity to an HDF5 group.

methods.name: from_hdf5
methods.docstring: Loads a quantity from an HDF5 dataset.

methods.name: with_uncertainty
methods.docstring: Returns a new Quantity with the specified uncertainty.

methods.name: plot
methods.docstring: Aesthetic plotting of this physical quantity.

methods.name: plot_slices
methods.docstring: Plots multiple 2D slice grids of a 3D+ Quantity for N-D field visualization.

methods.name: plot_interactive
methods.docstring: Creates an interactive GUI plot with sliders to scrub through N-D dimensions.

methods.name: plot_covariance
methods.docstring: Plots the covariance/correlation matrix of this Quantity if available.

methods.name: to
methods.docstring: Converts the quantity to a different unit or moves to a device.
